_meta.review_warning: AI-drafted v0. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION until reviewed by a qualified Vic WHS practitioner and approved.

hazards.title: Fall into excavation while setting forms
hazards.risk: Working at edge of pool excavation; depth 1.5–2.5m

controls.title: Temporary edge protection / cover at edges not actively being worked
Work from compacted bench at top of excavation, not from edge

hazards.title: Formwork collapse during pour
hazards.risk: Inadequately braced forms collapsing under wet concrete load (pool wall heights typical 1.2–1.6m)

controls.title: Formwork to engineer's design where wall height > 1.2m or non-standard geometry
Supervisor inspection of all bracing, ties, and joints before pour

hazards.title: Power tool hand injury (impact driver, saw)
hazards.risk: Slip / kickback when cutting forms or driving fasteners

controls.title: Correct tool for task; manufacturer's procedure

hazards.title: Manual handling — formwork panels
hazards.risk: Plywood sheets / form panels awkward, edge cuts, splinters

tasks: Pre-start: confirm excavation signed off; setout pegs in place; engineering drawings on site.
Set up cutting / lay-down area away from excavation edge.
Cut forms to size; pre-drill where required.
Lower forms into position from above; secure progressively.
Install bracing, ties, and walers per drawing.
Install penetrations for plumbing/electrical conduit per coordination drawings.
Apply form-release agent to all surfaces in contact with concrete.
Supervisor pre-pour inspection: dimensional check, brace integrity, tie torque, penetrations correct.
Sign off in Hub before reinforcement placement and pour.
